member.name: P:CodecMapper.Builder`2.App
member.summary: We evaluate the curried constructor from left to right against decoded field values stored in the compiler's `obj[]` buffer. This keeps the public DSL typed while still fitting the existing record compiler.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Builder`2
member.summary:
Builder state for the Pipeline DSL.
The builder keeps the constructor's remaining type in `'Ctor` so each appended field proves, at compile time, that one more argument has been supplied before `build` can close over the final record value.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Schema`1
member.summary:
A typed schema for values of `'T`.
Schemas are pure descriptions. Compile them with `Json.compile` or `Xml.compile` to get executable codecs.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.SchemaDefinition
member.summary: The structural schema shapes understood by the compiler backends.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.ISchema
member.summary:
Erased schema abstraction used internally and by advanced integrations.
Most callers should stay on `Schema<'T>`, but codecs and bridges compile against this untyped representation.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.SchemaField
member.summary:
Captures one named field inside a record schema.
A `SchemaField` binds the wire name, CLR type, getter, and nested schema for a single record member.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonValue
member.summary:
Represents arbitrary JSON when a contract cannot be lowered into a more precise schema.
This is the escape hatch for dynamic-key objects, heterogeneous arrays, and other JSON Schema shapes that do not fit the normal record/list/primitive model without losing parseability.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.WriteUInt64Digits(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Integer encoders share the same digit-emission loop, so keeping the helper on the concrete buffer avoids duplicating bounds checks and temporary allocations across the wider numeric writer surface.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.Return(System.Byte[])
member.summary: The .NET runtime rents arrays from the shared pool, while Fable keeps the simpler zero-allocation array semantics.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.Rent(System.Int32)
member.summary: Serialization buffers are short-lived scratch space, so renting from the shared pool removes most of the transient array allocation cost without changing the public string-returning API.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.Release
member.summary: Benchmarks and serializers should always return pooled storage after materializing the final string so hot-path allocation stays low.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.Create(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new buffer with the requested initial capacity.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer.Allocate(System.Int32)
member.summary: The codec writers overwrite every byte they publish, so on .NET we can skip zero-initializing fresh buffers and avoid the memset-heavy growth cost that shows up in profiling traces.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Core.ResizableBuffer
member.summary: Growable in-memory byte buffer used by the built-in codecs.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.Data
member.summary: Exposes the current backing storage.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.Count
member.summary: Exposes the number of written bytes.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteUInt64(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Writes a `uint64` value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteUInt32(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Writes a `uint32` value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteStringSlice(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: JSON escaping often needs to flush a slice of an existing string, and writing that slice directly avoids transient substring objects on the hot serialization path.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteString(System.String)
member.summary: Writes a UTF-8 string payload.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteInt64(System.Int64)
member.summary: Writes an `int64` value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteInt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes an integer value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteFloat(System.Double)
member.summary: Writes a `float` value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteDecimal(System.Decimal)
member.summary: Writes a `decimal` value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.WriteByte(System.Byte)
member.summary: Writes a single byte.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter.Ensure(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures that at least `n` more bytes can be written without reallocating.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Core.IByteWriter
member.summary: Abstraction for writing bytes, to be implemented per target platform.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource.SetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a copy of the source with an explicit absolute offset.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource.Advance(System.Int32)
member.summary: Advances the source by `n` bytes.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource
member.summary: A lightweight context for reading bytes.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.parseDecimalInvariantBytes(System.String,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Decimal parsing follows the same byte-level path so JSON numeric decode stays symmetric with the existing integer fast paths on .NET.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.parseFloatInvariantBytes(System.String,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: The handwritten JSON parser already identifies numeric token bounds in the UTF-8 source, so the .NET fast path can parse directly from bytes and avoid allocating transient strings for every float and decimal.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.parseUnsignedIntegerBytes(System.String,System.UInt64,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Unsigned integer decoding follows the same byte-level path but keeps syntax and range failures aligned with the string-based helpers.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.parseSignedIntegerBytes(System.String,System.UInt64,System.Boolean,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: JSON integer tokens are ASCII-only, so the .NET fast path can parse directly from source bytes and keep string allocation on the error path.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.classifyIntegerToken(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Integer parsing only needs ASCII digit validation, so we can classify invalid text versus range overflow without relying on exception types.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.isEmptyCollectionValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Field-policy wrappers sometimes need to distinguish explicit empty collections from non-empty ones without caring about the concrete type.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSourceModule.setOffset(System.Int32,CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource)
member.summary: Returns a copy of the source with an explicit absolute offset.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSourceModule.advance(System.Int32,CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource)
member.summary: Advances the source by `n` bytes.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Core.ByteSourceModule
member.summary: Functional helpers over `ByteSource`.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Core
member.summary: Low-level byte reading and writing primitives shared by the JSON and XML runtimes.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.build``1(CodecMapper.Builder{``0,``0})
member.summary: Closes a fully-applied pipeline and returns the schema for `'T`.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.fieldWith``3(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,``1},CodecMapper.Schema{``1},CodecMapper.Builder{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``1,``2}})
member.summary:
Adds a field with an explicit nested schema.
Use this when the field type needs a custom schema or should not rely on automatic resolution.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.field``3(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,``1},CodecMapper.Builder{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``1,``2}})
member.summary: Adds a field that can be resolved automatically from its type.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.construct``2(``0,CodecMapper.Builder{``1,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Captures the constructor used to rebuild `'T` during decoding.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.define``1
member.summary:
Pipeline DSL helpers.
The pipeline starts by capturing the curried constructor up front so subsequent field steps only describe the wire layout.
We still ask for `'T` explicitly because relying on field-label inference alone becomes brittle as soon as multiple record types share names like `Id` or `Name`.
Starts a pipeline schema definition for `'T`.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.resolveSchema(System.Type)
member.summary:
Resolves a built-in schema for a CLR type.
This powers automatic field resolution for primitives, F# collections, selected .NET collection interfaces, options, and the common built-in wrapper types.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.emptyStringAsNone(CodecMapper.Schema{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String}})
member.summary: Empty strings are often used as a legacy stand-in for "not provided". This wrapper keeps that conversion opt-in rather than weakening string option semantics globally.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.emptyCollectionAsValue``1(``0,CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Some config shapes use an explicit empty collection as "use the default collection" rather than as a meaningful distinct value.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.nullAsValue``1(``0,CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Config-style payloads sometimes use explicit `null` as "use the default value" for non-option fields. Keep that policy local.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.missingAsValue``1(``0,CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Config-style payloads sometimes have explicit defaults that should be applied only when a field is absent, not when it is present-but-invalid.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.missingAsNone``1(CodecMapper.Schema{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{``0}})
member.summary: Config-style payloads sometimes treat absent fields as "no value" instead of as a contract violation. Keep that policy explicit.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.jsonValue
member.summary:
Builds a schema for arbitrary JSON values.
This is the explicit fallback for imported contracts that cannot be represented as a deterministic record/array/primitive schema without a separate normalization or validation step.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.option``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Builds a schema for an option value.
The default semantics are strict: `None` is explicit on the wire, and missing fields still fail unless you add a field-policy wrapper.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.collection``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Builds a schema for `ICollection<'T>`.
This preserves the normal array wire shape while interoperating with common mutable collection interfaces from .NET APIs.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.readOnlyList``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Builds a schema for `IReadOnlyList<'T>`.
Keep the wire form identical to arrays while still allowing .NET-facing APIs to expose read-only collection interfaces.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.resizeArray``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Builds a schema for `ResizeArray<'T>` / `List<T>`.
.NET interop often surfaces mutable list shapes even when the wire contract is just a homogeneous JSON or XML array.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.array``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Builds a schema for an array.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.nonEmptyList``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Some wire contracts require at least one item, but keeping that rule in the schema is still clearer than scattering ad hoc list checks elsewhere.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.list``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Builds a schema for an F# list.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.formatFloat(System.Double)
member.summary: Keep the .NET path using round-trip float formatting, but let Fable use the host number string form instead of rejecting the `"R"` specifier.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.timeSpan
member.summary: A schema for `TimeSpan` using the invariant `"c"` format.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.dateTimeOffset
member.summary: A schema for `DateTimeOffset` using the round-trippable `"O"` string format.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.dateTime
member.summary: A schema for `DateTime` using the round-trippable `"O"` string format.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.char
member.summary: A schema for `char` backed by a single-character string.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.guid
member.summary:
A schema for `Guid` using the round-trippable `"D"` string format.
Common domain identity and timestamp types ride on top of the string codec so JSON and XML stay symmetric without extra parser branches.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.uint16
member.summary: A schema for `uint16`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.sbyte
member.summary: A schema for `sbyte`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.byte
member.summary: A schema for `byte`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.int16
member.summary: A schema for `int16`.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.rangedInt``1(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.Int32,``0},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,System.Int32})
member.summary: Narrow numeric types can safely reuse the integer codec as long as the schema enforces range checks on decode.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.positiveInt
member.summary: Positive identifiers and counters are a common wire-level constraint, and `tryMap` keeps that rule opt-in rather than global.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.trimmedString
member.summary: Some contracts normalize surrounding whitespace at the boundary rather than making every caller remember to trim before encode and after decode.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.nonEmptyString
member.summary: Empty strings are often accidental placeholders rather than meaningful values. This helper keeps that validation explicit and reusable.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.tryMap``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpResult{``1,System.String}},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``1,``0},CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Smart constructors need a way to reject decoded values without forcing callers to smuggle exceptions through plain `map`.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.map``2(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``0,``1},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{``1,``0},CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Projects an existing schema through total wrap and unwrap functions.
Use this when the wire shape is unchanged but the in-memory model uses a wrapper type.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.bool
member.summary: A schema for `bool`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.string
member.summary: A schema for `string`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.decimal
member.summary: A schema for `decimal`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.float
member.summary: A schema for `float`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.uint64
member.summary: A schema for `uint64`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.uint32
member.summary: A schema for `uint32`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.int64
member.summary: A schema for `int64`.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Schema.int
member.summary: A schema for `int`.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.create``1(CodecMapper.SchemaDefinition)
member.summary:
Creates a schema from a raw structural definition.
This is public for advanced integrations, but most callers should prefer the higher-level helpers in this module.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Schema.unwrap(CodecMapper.ISchema)
member.summary: Exposes the erased schema view for advanced integrations.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Schema
member.summary:
Helpers for building explicit schemas and reusing common built-in shapes.
This is the main authoring surface for `CodecMapper`. Build a `Schema<'T>` once, then compile it into JSON or XML codecs.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Json.Codec`1
member.summary: A compiled JSON codec for a specific schema.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Json.Decoder`1
member.summary: Decoder shape used by the compiled JSON runtime.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Json.JsonWriter
member.summary: The byte-level output abstraction used by JSON encoders.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Json.JsonSource
member.summary: The byte-level input state for JSON decoding.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.deserializeBytes``1(CodecMapper.Json.Codec{``0},System.Byte[])
member.summary: Deserializes a UTF-8 byte payload using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.deserialize``1(CodecMapper.Json.Codec{``0},System.String)
member.summary:
Deserializes a JSON payload using a previously compiled codec.
The entire payload must be consumed. Trailing content is treated as an error rather than ignored.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.serialize``1(CodecMapper.Json.Codec{``0},``0)
member.summary: Serializes a value to JSON using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.codec``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: `codec` remains as the shorter schema-to-codec alias for callers that prefer the direct `compile schema` shape without the longer name.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.buildAndCompile``1(CodecMapper.Builder{``0,``0})
member.summary: Inline schema pipelines read more clearly when the final `build` and JSON compile step collapse into one terminal pipeline stage.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.compile``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Compiles a schema into a reusable JSON codec.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.Json.defaultSerializeBufferCapacity
member.summary: Tiny payloads fit comfortably in the old `128`-byte buffer, but the benchmark runner now measures batches of `100` records where repeated growth and copy steps dominate allocation churn. Starting larger keeps the hot JSON path closer to realistic message sizes.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.Runtime.returnObjectArray(System.Object[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Record field buffers may hold arbitrary user objects, so return them cleared to avoid keeping payload graphs alive across runs.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.Runtime.rentObjectArray(System.Int32)
member.summary: Record decode still needs temporary erased storage today, but pooling the `obj[]` buffers removes one of the largest remaining allocation sources on nested decode workloads.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.Runtime.makeList(System.Type,System.Object[])
member.summary: XML shares the same erased list-construction helper, so keep the old entrypoint as a thin wrapper over the cached builder.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Json.Runtime.decodeAtPath``1(CodecMapper.Json.DecodePathSegment,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource,System.ValueTuple{``0,CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource}},CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource)
member.summary: The hot decode path already knows the field or item segment up front, so accepting the decoder directly avoids closure allocation around every successful nested decode.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Json
member.summary:
JSON codec compilation and runtime helpers.
Compile a schema once, then reuse the resulting codec for repeated JSON serialization and deserialization.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportReport
member.summary:
Describes the outcome of importing a JSON Schema into `Schema<JsonValue>`.
The imported schema always decodes through the raw JSON DOM fallback. These diagnostics explain which keywords were actively enforced and which keywords caused the importer to stay on the raw-fallback path.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptions
member.summary:
Options for JSON Schema import.
Import stays structural by default, but callers can opt into extra string-format checks by registering validators for specific `format` names.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.FormatValidator
member.summary: Validates a JSON Schema `format` string against domain-specific rules.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.import(System.String)
member.summary:
Imports the deterministic JSON Schema subset into a JSON-only `Schema<JsonValue>`.
This uses the built-in format validator set. Use `importUsing` when you need project-specific `format` validation behavior.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.importUsing(CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptions,System.String)
member.summary:
Imports the deterministic JSON Schema subset into a JSON-only `Schema<JsonValue>`.
This is the convenience entrypoint when you only need the imported schema itself. Use `importWithReport` when you also need diagnostics about which keywords were enforced or left on the raw-fallback path.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.importWithReport(System.String)
member.summary:
Imports JSON Schema into `Schema<JsonValue>` and returns diagnostics.
This uses the built-in format validator set. Use `importWithReportUsing` when you need project-specific `format` rules.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.importWithReportUsing(CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptions,System.String)
member.summary:
Imports the deterministic JSON Schema subset into a JSON-only `Schema<JsonValue>`.
The imported schema keeps the original JSON shape by decoding through `Schema.jsonValue` first, then applying the supported JSON Schema rules as structural refinement over the raw DOM. Unsupported branch-shaping features currently fall back to the unconstrained raw JSON schema.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.generate``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Generates a compact JSON Schema document for the JSON wire shape of a schema.
The exported schema describes the JSON contract only. XML-specific shape details and business-rule semantics inside mapping functions are not representable here and therefore export as the underlying wire form.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptionsModule.defaults
member.summary: Built-in validators for the most common round-trippable string formats.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptionsModule.withFormat(System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpResult{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,System.String}},CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptions)
member.summary: Adds or replaces one `format` validator.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptionsModule.empty
member.summary: No custom format validators.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonSchema.ImportOptionsModule
member.summary: Helpers for building JSON Schema import options.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.JsonSchema
member.summary:
JSON Schema export for the JSON wire shape described by `Schema<'T>`.
This exports the structural JSON contract implied by the schema. Mapping wrappers such as `Schema.map` and `Schema.tryMap` contribute the underlying wire shape, while field-policy wrappers affect whether object properties are listed as required.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Xml.Codec`1
member.summary: A compiled XML codec for a specific schema.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Xml.XmlWriter
member.summary: The byte-level output abstraction used by XML encoders.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Xml.XmlSource
member.summary: The byte-level input state for XML decoding.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.deserializeBytes``1(CodecMapper.Xml.Codec{``0},System.Byte[])
member.summary: Deserializes a UTF-8 byte payload using a previously compiled XML codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.deserialize``1(CodecMapper.Xml.Codec{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes an XML payload using the schema-derived root element name.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.serialize``1(CodecMapper.Xml.Codec{``0},``0)
member.summary: Serializes a value to XML using the schema-derived root element name.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.codec``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: `codec` mirrors `Json.codec` for callers that still prefer the direct schema-to-codec alias over the longer `compile` name.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.buildAndCompile``1(CodecMapper.Builder{``0,``0})
member.summary: Inline schema pipelines read more clearly when the final `build` and XML compile step collapse into one terminal pipeline stage.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.compile``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Compiles a schema into a reusable XML codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.Runtime.escapeText(System.String)
member.summary: Text nodes must escape structural characters or the decoder cannot distinguish content from markup.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Xml.Runtime.expectOpenTag(System.String,CodecMapper.Core.ByteSource)
member.summary: The XML surface is intentionally small: element tags and escaped text nodes, with no attributes or mixed content.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Xml
member.summary:
XML codec compilation and runtime helpers.
The XML backend intentionally supports a smaller explicit subset than the JSON backend: element content only, repeated `<item>` nodes for collections, and ignorable inter-element whitespace.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.KeyValue.Codec`1
member.summary: A compiled flat key/value codec for a specific schema.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.KeyValue.Options
member.summary: Options controlling how flattened keys are named.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.deserializeSeq``1(CodecMapper.KeyValue.Codec{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Tuple{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Deserializes any sequence of key/value pairs by first normalizing it to a map.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.deserialize``1(CodecMapper.KeyValue.Codec{``0},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Deserializes a flat key/value map using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.serialize``1(CodecMapper.KeyValue.Codec{``0},``0)
member.summary: Serializes a value to a flat key/value map using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.codec``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: `codec` remains as the shorter schema-to-codec alias for callers that prefer the direct compile step under the default options.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.buildAndCompile``1(CodecMapper.Builder{``0,``0})
member.summary: Inline schema pipelines read more clearly when the final `build` and key/value compile step collapse into one terminal pipeline stage.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.compile``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Compiles a schema into a reusable flat key/value codec using dotted keys.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.KeyValue.compileUsing``1(CodecMapper.KeyValue.Options,CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: Compiles a schema into a reusable flat key/value codec using explicit options.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.KeyValue.OptionsModule.environment
member.summary: Environment-variable style naming using `__` separators and upper-case keys.

member.name: P:CodecMapper.KeyValue.OptionsModule.defaults
member.summary: Default flat key naming using dotted paths such as `parent.child`.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.KeyValue
member.summary:
Flat `string,string` contract projection for config-style schemas.
This backend is intentionally narrower than JSON or XML: it targets flattened key/value surfaces such as app settings or environment variables.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Yaml.deserialize``1(CodecMapper.Yaml.Codec{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes a YAML payload using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Yaml.serialize``1(CodecMapper.Yaml.Codec{``0},``0)
member.summary: Serializes a value to YAML using a previously compiled codec.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Yaml.codec``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary: `codec` mirrors the other format modules for callers that still prefer the shorter schema-to-codec alias over the longer `compile` name.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Yaml.buildAndCompile``1(CodecMapper.Builder{``0,``0})
member.summary: Inline schema pipelines read more clearly when the final `build` and YAML compile step collapse into one terminal pipeline stage.

member.name: M:CodecMapper.Yaml.compile``1(CodecMapper.Schema{``0})
member.summary:
Compiles a schema into a reusable YAML codec.
The YAML surface is intentionally small and config-oriented. It reuses the compiled JSON codec and a `JsonValue` projection instead of adding a second full schema compiler.

member.name: T:CodecMapper.Yaml
member.summary:
YAML projection layered on top of the portable `JsonValue` model.
This intentionally supports a small YAML subset suitable for config-style contracts: mappings, sequences, scalars, `null`, and quoted/plain strings.
